title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell sharply after reports that President Trump is considering rolling back some tariffs on aluminum and steel products. Traders reacted to concerns that the duties are hurting consumers, while broader weakness hit other base metals as well.

title: Aluminum Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows aluminum prices rising month over month and significantly higher year over year. The data indicates aluminum was at 3,599.85 USD/mt in April 2026, reflecting continued strength in the market.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports aluminum rising to 3,650.90 USD/T, its highest level since March 2022. The commodity has gained over the past month and is up strongly over the last 12 months, signaling a sustained uptrend.

title: The Outlook For Aluminum, Steel & Copper Prices
summary: J.P. Morgan’s outlook discusses aluminum pricing expectations and broader market drivers, including demand and trade policy. The report notes that future U.S. copper tariffs may also affect base metals markets.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows aluminum futures trading around 3,650.90 with a slight change from the previous close. The page provides live pricing and intraday trading context for the aluminum market.

title: Aluminum Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
summary: S&P Global offers market pricing, analysis, and forward-looking intelligence for aluminum. The page is a market reference for traders and analysts tracking metals trends.

title: Live Price of Aluminium per Ounce
summary: Markets Insider provides live aluminum pricing and historical snapshots. The listing is useful for tracking short-term moves and comparing current levels with recent highs and lows.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity, with Fox News reporting the move in the context of President Donald Trump's tariffs. The article highlights the company's production increase as a sign of renewed momentum in U.S. aluminum output and capacity utilization.

title: A Roadmap for Next Generation Supply Chain Resilience
summary: This white paper says the U.S. primary aluminum segment is operating at 53% of installed capacity and argues that rebuilding domestic aluminum production is needed to meet demand. It outlines three ways to expand capacity: new primary production, restarting or expanding existing facilities, and increasing remelt capacity.

title: Smelter closure to wipe nearly 30% of US aluminum capacity
summary: This report says a smelter closure will eliminate nearly 30% of U.S. aluminum capacity. It underscores the continuing pressure on domestic aluminum production and the vulnerability of U.S. supply capacity.

title: HRC Steel reaches highest level since December 2023
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel prices climbed to 1,161 USD/T on May 22, 2026, marking the highest level since December 2023. Trading Economics notes that HRC steel has continued to trend higher over the past month and year, signaling persistent upward pressure in the market.

title: Steel price trends continue to move higher amid constrained supply and improving demand
summary: Ryerson reports that steel prices are continuing to rise across flat, plate, and long products. The update attributes the increase to constrained supply and improving demand, suggesting a firm near-term pricing environment.

title: Steel price rises to 3,171 CNY/T on May 22, 2026
summary: Trading Economics says steel prices increased to 3,171 CNY/T on May 22, 2026, up slightly from the prior day. The data also shows gains over the past month and year, indicating continued strength in the broader steel market.

title: US domestic steel prices continue to increase amid resilient demand and tight supply
summary: Steel Market Update says domestic steel prices in the U.S. have kept rising for most products. The outlet points to resilient demand and tight supply, with sheet and plate prices increasing as market conditions remain supportive.

title: Copper Prices Outlook
summary: Copper prices have rebounded to around $13,000/mt, but the market remains sensitive to geopolitical developments and growth concerns tied to the Iran conflict. J.P. Morgan notes that improving Chinese micro fundamentals are helping, though copper still appears exposed and has a medium-term support zone around $11,100–$11,200/mt.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper rose to $6.35 per pound on May 22, 2026, gaining 1.41% from the previous day. Trading Economics also reports that copper is up 4.33% over the past month and 31.38% compared with a year earlier, reflecting a strong recent uptrend.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com lists copper futures at 6.3790, above the previous close of 6.2940. The page provides live pricing and market data, but the search snippet does not include a specific news story or commentary on recent market trends.

title: Copper supply gap to widen 24% by 2040 as electrification accelerates
summary: A new study says global copper supply is set to tighten as electrification drives demand higher. Without major new investment, production could peak around 2030 and then decline, leaving a widening gap by 2040 even after accounting for increased recycling.

title: Why the US Is Pushing to Rebuild Its Copper Supply
summary: This video discusses why rebuilding domestic copper capacity has become an economic and strategic priority for the United States. It references new and expanding capacity in Augusta, Georgia, as part of the broader push.

title: Copper Production and Development in Western USA
summary: The article reviews recent trends in U.S. copper output and project development in the western states. It notes that domestic mined copper production fell in 2023, reaching a five-year low, and highlights the importance of new mines and expansions.

title: Global Trade Update (May 2025): Focus on critical minerals
summary: UNCTAD's update highlights concentration in the global copper market, including the geographic distribution of reserves and mining output. The report points to Chile's leading role and the Democratic Republic of the Congo's rising importance in copper production.

title: US miners eye copper comeback as demand rises
summary: The article says U.S. copper demand is expected to double over the next decade, raising concerns about domestic supply. It argues that production increases and policy changes will be needed if the U.S. wants to keep up with future demand.

title: The U.S. Copper Industry Needs a Strategy, Not a Contradiction
summary: WRI examines the gap between U.S. copper consumption and domestic supply, including mining and recycling. The piece argues that a coherent long-term strategy is needed to support a more resilient copper supply chain.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: This article discusses how copper is becoming increasingly central to the clean energy transition, with demand projected to rise sharply as electrification expands. It highlights the need for innovation in supply, recycling, and production to support sustainability goals while meeting growing demand.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: This piece examines the growing importance of copper in the energy transition and how companies are responding to rising demand. It emphasizes sustainability efforts, production scaling, and digital innovation as key strategies for the copper industry.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: This article focuses on initiatives aimed at increasing sustainable copper production across the value chain. It highlights technological and process innovations intended to improve resource efficiency and address challenges in copper supply.

title: Copper facts
summary: This resource summarizes copper's growing role in the green energy transition and its importance in electricity networks and clean technologies. It supports the broader theme of copper demand rising alongside sustainability and electrification trends.

title: Nickel rose to 18,880 USD/T on May 22, 2026
summary: Nickel prices edged higher, reaching 18,880 USD/T, up 0.67% from the previous day. The chart and historical data indicate the metal has also risen over the past month and year, suggesting continued strength in the market.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows nickel futures trading near 18,878.88, slightly above the previous close of 18,673.13. The page provides live pricing, charts, and market data for tracking short-term moves in nickel.

title: LME Nickel
summary: The London Metal Exchange page offers current and historical nickel prices, stocks, and monthly averages. It is a key reference for tracking official market benchmarks and inventory trends.

title: Nickel Prices April 2026 update
summary: IMARC's pricing report shows mixed regional nickel pricing in April 2026, with Northeast Asia, Europe, and North America all posting declines versus the prior period. The report suggests some recent softness despite the broader higher-price environment.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has reportedly ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world’s biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce output as part of efforts to lift global nickel prices. The mine’s annual ore quota is said to be cut to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025, though the company plans to seek a revision.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia’s energy minister confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while also cracking down on environmentally harmful operations. The move marks a reversal from prior mining quota policy aimed at regulating supply volatility.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures rose back above $18,800 per ton after reports of additional output reductions, following a recent dip to near a four-week low. The price move reflects ongoing market sensitivity to supply-side developments.
